item.title: Review of DDA laws timely
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Constitutional Law Reform Commission (CLRC) will review the laws on DDA

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts: Independence statement from the President of Bougainville
item.description: [Bougainville News] My fellow Bougainvilleans, In the next few days, the resolve of the 97.8 percent of Bougainvilleans who voted for Bougainville to attain independence will be tested by the tabling of the Bi-Partisan Report on the 2019 Bougainville Referendum in the … Continue reading →

item.title: USP climbs more than 400 places in Times Higher Education Sustainability Impact Rankings and secures Top 10 World University Innovation category ranking
item.description: [Education News PNG] The University of the South Pacific (USP) has strengthened its standing among the world’s internationally recognized universities after climbing more than 400 places in the The post USP climbs more than 400 places in Times Higher Education Sustainability Impact Rankings and secures Top 10 World Univ

item.title: PNG Restricts Australian Poultry Imports After Bird Flu Discovery
item.description: [Inside PNG] NAQIA restricts Australian poultry imports after H5N1 bird flu found in WA wild birds. Free-range WA products suspended whilst barn-raised are safe. The post PNG Restricts Australian Poultry Imports After Bird Flu Discovery first appeared on Inside PNG . The post PNG Restricts Australian Poultry Imp

item.title: Newmont PNG expands trade training with national accreditation
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Newmont PNG has reached another milestone in workforce development after its Lihir Technical Training Centre secured national accreditation to conduct onsite Belt Splicing trade testing, giving more Papua New Guineans access to recognised trade qualifications without leaving the mine site. Newmont P

item.title: Monetisation of Online Content in Papua New Guinea
item.description: [Pawa TV] The monetisation of online content in Papua New Guinea is growing, with efforts to enhance infrastructure and access for creators on platforms like YouTube, Facebook, and Instagram amid policy challenges. The post Monetisation of Online Content in Papua New Guinea first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: PNG Defence Force Personnel Complete Trade Training Under Australia Partnership
item.description: [PNG Facts] Thirty-five members of the PNG Defence Force (PNGDF) have graduated from an eight-week vocational training program, equipping them with practical automotive and carpentry skills to strengthen the organisation's technical workforce. PNG Defence Force Personnel Complete Trade Training Under Australia

item.title: Nakanai Advances Plans for New Level 4 District Hospital
item.description: [Post Courier] The Nakanai District Administration has continued discussions with the National Department of Health (NDOH) on the proposed establishment of a Level 4 District Hospital at Bialla, marking another important step towards improving healthcare services in the district. To access this post, you must purc

item.title: Censorship Office destroys 645 prohibited items
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By GEORGINA MICHAEL The Office of Censorship has destroyed 645 prohibited items today, reaffirming its commitment to enforcing the Classification of Publication (Censorship) Act 1989. The items were confiscated during enforcement operations conducted throughout last year in partnership with the Papu

item.title: No more looking for stages: PNG Chiefs sign Morea, Glare and Voro
item.description: [The PNG Sun] It is a proud moment for Papua New Guinea (PNG) rugby league! The PNG Chiefs have announced their first-ever development signings, with Gairo Voro, Morea Morea and Finley Glare joining the club ahead of its NRL debut in 2028. Related news: https://pnghauslain.com/3-png-born-players-signed-on-with-pn

item.title: Mt. Bagana na El Niño givim hevi long Bogenvil
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Maunten paia long Mt. Bagana wantaim drai bilong El Nino i kamapim planti hevi long ol manmeri long Bogenvil. Ol i sot long gutpela wara na kaikai long ples we i stap klostu long we asis bilong maunten i pundaun.

item.title: PNG and the Failure of EUDR Benchmarking: When “Low Risk” Ignores Reality
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Papua New Guinea’s designation as “low risk” in the EUDR may now be one of the clearest examples of the disconnect with reality.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts :Bougainville Landowners demand mining companies halt reopening of copper pit
item.description: [Bougainville News] Traditional landowners are fighting back against plans to reopen an existing Bougainville copper mine which has remained dormant for nearly four decades. During a public event on Bougainville Island last week a warning was declared against the companies who landowners … Continue reading →

item.title: Ten (10) Arrested Including Two (2) Chinese Nationals Over Alleged Murder Of Young PNG Man
item.description: [Inside PNG] Police in Port Moresby have arrested ten suspects over the death of a 20 year old man and the attempted murder of another victim. Among the arrests were four security guards from Fix Security Firm, four shop assistants and two Chinese expatriates from Cash and Carry Shopping Center at Gerehu’s Rainb

item.title: PNG Firm Brave Group Partners Australian Company to Launch New Integrity and Security Division
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Brave Group Ltd CEO Wesley Roy Papua New Guinea is set to strengthen its governance and professional standards after PNG-owned Brave Group (PNG) Ltd entered into a strategic partnership with Australia's Pinnacle Integrity Pty Ltd to establish the country's first integrated Integrity, Cultural Risk a

item.title: Women in PNG Politics
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea's political history highlights persistent gender inequality, with women facing challenges in attaining parliamentary representation, despite various reform efforts and initiatives aimed at increasing their visibility and participation. The post Women in PNG Politics first appeared o

item.title: PNG Democracy Can Recover Under New Electoral Commissioner, Says Ipatas
item.description: [PNG Facts] Enga Governor Chief Sir Peter Ipatas Enga Governor Chief Sir Peter Ipatas says Papua New Guinea has endured too many disappointing elections, but believes the country has an opportunity to restore public confidence following the appointment of Dr Nicole Haley as Electoral Commissioner. Throwing his

item.title: CEFI advance financial inclusion through partnership
item.description: [Post Courier] The Centre for Excellence in Financial Inclusion (CEFI), the Department for Community Development and Religion (DFCDR), and Women’s Micro Bank (WMB) have reaffirmed their commitment to expanding financial inclusion and economic empowerment across Papua New Guinea through the signing of two Memoranda

item.title: Tabitha Suwae appointed Acting Chief Ombudsman
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] PRIME Minister James Marape has announced the appointment of Ms Tabitha Suwae as Acting Chief Ombudsman following a decision by the Ombudsman Appointments Committee (OAC). Ms Suwae, who has served as an Ombudsman since October 2024, will lead the Ombudsman Commission in an acting capacity while the

item.title: Connect PNG Investment intensifies as GG executes K1.1 billion West Sepik Road contracts
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Nearly three months after having inked the K1.97 billion missing link road project connecting Port Moresby to Alotau via Abau, Governor-General, H.E. Grand Chief Sir Bob Bofeng Dadae, has executed two major road construction contracts worth more than K1.1 billion for West Sepik Province under the Go

item.title: Pisin mekim na balus bungim hevi long Goroka
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Ol pisin i bin pasim ranwe long Goroka ples balus na i givim hevi long wanpela balus bilong PNG Air. Tupela wil bilong balus i bruk tasol no gat manmeri i kisim bagarap long dispela hevi.

item.title: Sukundimi Walks Before Me nominated for awards
item.description: [Act Now PNG] The striking documentary 'Sukundimi Walks Before Me' has been nominated for both the Documentary Australia Award for Best Documentary and the Sustainable Future Award

item.title: Bougainville News Alert : Statement from the Office of the President: Response to Chief John L Momis, GCL, former President of Bougainville
item.description: [Bougainville News] It is disappointing to see a leader of John Momis’ stature, a former two term President of Bougainville and longtime Regional Member for Bougainville trying to undermine the good work of the Autonomous Bougainville Government. See Momis Press Release Part … Continue reading →

item.description: [Education News PNG] Western Highlands, Papua New Guinea – Classes at Paglum Adventist Secondary School in Western Highlands Province have been suspended for one week following a violent The post Fight at Paglum Adventist Secondary School Leaves 16 Students Injured, Classes Suspended first appeared on Education News PNG

item.title: PNG names Dr Nicole Haley to lead Electoral Commission
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Prime Minister James Marape has announced Dr Nicole Haley as Papua New Guinea's new Electoral Commissioner, entrusting the election specialist with overseeing preparations for the country's 2027 National General Election. The appointment follows weeks of speculation over the position and comes after

item.title: Tourism in PNG Compared to Fiji
item.description: [Pawa TV] Fiji significantly leads in tourism development over Papua New Guinea, generating USD 2.5 billion compared to PNG's USD 182.94 million, highlighting the need for PNG to improve accessibility and marketing. The post Tourism in PNG Compared to Fiji first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.description: [PNG Facts] PNGTUC Assistant Secretary General, Anton Sekum The PNG Trade Union Congress is calling for greater transparency over the appointment of foreign nationals to senior public service positions, saying the government should clearly explain the roles, responsibilities and terms of engagement for expatria

item.description: [Post Courier] Prime Minister James Marape has announced the appointment of Ms Tabitha Suwae as Acting Chief Ombudsman following a decision by the Ombudsman Appointments Committee (OAC). item.title: Government commits K50,000 towards PNG Kickboxing Federation
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By GABRIEL KATUHO THE Government of Papua New Guinea has made a commitment of K50 000 towards the PNG Kickboxing Federation at the Taurama Aquatics Centre in Port Moresby on Sunday 28th of June 2026. Prime Minster James Marape and Governor for New Ireland province Byron Chan made their appearance at

item.title: PM Marape says PNG Chiefs will drive National unity, strengthen Pacific ties through Rugby League
item.description: [The PNG Sun] PNG Prime Minister James Marape says Papua New Guinea’s entry into the Australian National Rugby League in 2028 through the PNG Chiefs will extend beyond sport, creating opportunities for national unity, tourism and regional cooperation. The Prime Minister made the remarks on Tuesday following a mee

item.title: Aropa na Kieta seif long bom nau
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Ol pipel long Central Bougainville i no ken wari tumas long ol bom bilong Wol Woa 2 nau bihain long ABG i wokbung wantaim New Zealand Defence Force long rausim ol.

item.title: Illegal logging will sink government's timber processing plans
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Government plan to establish four regional centres for timber processing will fail unless it first puts an end to widespread illegal logging

item.description: [Bougainville News] FIVE YEARS ago we farewelled GC Sir Michael Somare with the most deserving tributes around the country. We also paused to remember him as someone whose political life is synonymous with the birth of PNG as a nation state … Continue reading →

item.title: PNG Government backs review to protect SME funding integrity
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] PNG Government backs review to protect SME funding integrity The PNG Government has confirmed a review of its flagship SME Support Programme following concerns raised over the administration of K200 million in funding designed to support small business development nationwide. The initiative is part

item.title: Early Campaigning in PNG
item.description: [Pawa TV] Concerns grow in Papua New Guinea over early campaigning, which breaches electoral laws. Enforcement is weak, with limited consequences for offenders, raising issues about electoral integrity and public trust. The post Early Campaigning in PNG first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: PNG Chiefs Secure Three Kumuls Stars Ahead of Landmark 2028 Debut
item.description: [PNG Facts] PNG Chiefs Secure Three Kumuls Stars Ahead of Landmark 2028 Debut The PNG Chiefs have strengthened their squad for their inaugural 2028 campaign by securing the signatures of PNG LNG Kumuls representatives Morea Morea, Gairo Voro and Finley Glare. The announcement brings together three players who h

item.title: Bougainville Cost Report released
item.description: [Post Courier] Bougainville has revealed its Cost of Services Report, estimating K1.3 billion is needed annually to run government, almost double its current budget of K751.47 million. item.title: 23 Simbu Police Officers Undergo Leadership Training
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] TWENTY-THREE Sergeants (All Class) and Constables (All Class) from the Simbu Provincial Police Command are undergoing training for Sergeants and Non-Commissioned Officers (NCOs) to strengthen their understanding of police administration and improve their ability to uphold accountability and responsi

item.title: PM Marape orders independent audit of K200 million SME Funding Programme
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Prime Minister James Marape has ordered an independent audit into the Government’s SME funding programme following public allegations over fund management, while reaffirming continued support for small business development. The Prime Minister said concerns raised publicly over the administration and

item.title: Graun bruk katim Sepik Haiwe
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Bikpela ren i mekim graun i bruk na bagarapim rot long Sepik Haiwe. Dispela rot i save bungim Is na Wes Sepik na i go olgeta long boda bilong PNG wantaim Indonesia.

item.title: Illegal logging will sink government’s downstream processing plans
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Government’s ambitious plan to establish four regional centres for timber processing and value-adding will fail unless it first puts an end to widespread illegal logging

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : Simon Pentanu : My memories of Kieta
item.description: [Bougainville News] The first permanent German presence in Kieta, Bougainville, began with the establishment of a Marist mission in 1901, followed by an official German colonial administrative office in 1905 to oversee the region. They would have seen, steaming into the deep natural harbour, … Continue reading →

item.title: PNG Health Access Strengthened Through Australia Aviation Partnership

item.title: Cancer Treatment in PNG
item.description: [Pawa TV] Cancer poses a significant health challenge in Papua New Guinea, exacerbated by late diagnoses, limited screening, and inadequate treatment facilities. Strengthening oncology services and prevention efforts is essential to improve outcomes. The post Cancer Treatment in PNG first appeared on PAWA TV

item.title: PNG Minister Daki Faces Renewed Resignation Calls Over K200 Million SME Scheme
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea Commerce and Industry Minister Win Bakri Daki is facing renewed pressure to resign after Opposition Leader James Nomane intensified calls for an independent investigation into the Government's K200 million Small and Medium Enterprise (SME) funding scheme, arguing that key questions

item.title: Chimbu Division of Education rolls out SBC training to all teachers
item.description: [Post Courier] Teachers from Primary, High, Secondary and TVET institutions across Chimbu Province have begun both the National In-Service Training 2026 and the Provincial In-Service Training, as the Division of Education rolls out orientation of the Standard-Based Curriculum from 29th June to 3rd July. To access

item.title: Tribal Foundation Demands Arrests After Attack on Rights Defender
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] PNG Tribal Foundation President GT Bustin has condemned the alleged attack on one of the frontline staff by angry locals in Banz on Tuesday night (30/06/26) and is calling for an immediate police investigation and the arrest of the suspect in Jiwaka Province. Mr. Bustin condemned the violent attack

item.title: PNG shifts Coconut Industry to high-value products to boost rural incomes
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Papua New Guinea is repositioning its coconut industry toward high-value manufacturing and downstream processing in a move aimed at creating jobs, increasing rural incomes and attracting investment during the country’s 50th Independence year. Speaking this week at the National Agriculture Research S

item.title: Gavman Minista bam long kar long haiwe
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Minista bilong 'Energy' na Memba bilong Kairuku, Peter Namea Isoaimo i bin bam long kar long 2nd Februeri, 2026 long Hiritano Haiwe. Em i bam long kar wantaim wanpela soldia bilong PNG Defence Force (PNGDF). Ol kar bilong tupela man i stap nau long polis stesin long 15 Mile na wok painimaut i gohet

item.title: PNG Aviation Network to Benefit from New Niusky Investments and Leadership
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Papua New Guinea's air traffic management system is poised for further improvement as Niusky Pacific Limited advances plans to modernise its national aviation infrastructure under a newly appointed board of directors. With fresh leadership now in place, the state-owned air navigation service provide

item.title: The Story of the Meri Blouse
item.description: [Pawa TV] A Symbol of PNG Identity. The meri blouse, locally also called kolos in Tok Pisin, is one of the most recognisable garments associated with women in Papua New Guinea (PNG). Featuring a loose, comfortable fit often crafted from colourful fabric with bold motifs, it serves not just as clothing but as

item.title: PNG appoints Dr Nicole Haley as new Electoral Commissioner
item.description: [PNG Facts] Dr Nicole Haley Papua New Guinea has appointed respected academic Dr Nicole Haley as the country's new Electoral Commissioner, ending weeks of public anticipation over who would take charge of one of the nation's most important constitutional offices ahead of the 2027 National General Election. Prim

item.title: PNG office of Censorship destroys 645 prohibited items
item.description: [Post Courier] ​The Office of Censorship has successfully destroyed 645 prohibited items confiscated during enforcement operations conducted throughout 2025, reaffirming its commitment to protecting the public and enforcing the Classification of Publication (Censorship) Act 1989. item.title: PNG MSME Council Appoints Jeffrey Singer as Executive Director
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] THE PNG Micro Small and Medium Enterprise (MSME) Council Inc. has appointed Mr Jeffrey Singer as its new Executive Director, marking a key step in strengthening the organisation’s leadership and expanding support services for micro, small and medium enterprises across Papua New Guinea. The appointme

item.title: Sealed Erima Mambu road opens as Governor Parkop commits to more developments
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Residents of Erima in Port Moresby are set to benefit from further infrastructure upgrades after NCD Governor Powes Parkop officially opened the newly resealed Erima Mambu street and committed to completing remaining development works. The opening ceremony was attended by Moresby North-East MP John

item.description: [Bougainville News] Meeting Francis Ona – from my Panguna journals. My six year appointment as Chief Ombudsman that began in January 1995 ended on 31 December 2000. This time was also the end of my national public service career. It was … Continue reading →

The latest Hostplus Cup ladder paints a worrying picture, with the Hunters sitting at the bottom of the 15-team competition after another disappointing campaign. While rival clubs c

item.title: Youth Unemployment in PNG
item.description: [Pawa TV] Youth unemployment in Papua New Guinea poses significant socio-economic challenges, with many school leavers struggling to find formal employment. Despite government initiatives, structural barriers persist, risking long-term underemployment and poverty. The post Youth Unemployment in PNG first appe

item.title: PNG Government orders review into SME funding programme amid concerns
item.description: [PNG Facts] The PNG Government has moved to tighten oversight of its Small and Medium Enterprise (SME) Support Programme following growing concerns over how funds intended for local entrepreneurs have been administered. The review is aimed at restoring confidence in a programme central to the country’s economic

item.title: ON THIS DAY IN OUR HISTORY: Flying out after 40 years
item.description: [Post Courier] A man who has spent 40 years in PNG was flown to Australia yesterday for urgent medical treatment. The post ON THIS DAY IN OUR HISTORY: Flying out after 40 years appeared first on Post Courier .

item.title: Marape Commends PNG Kickboxing Federation
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] PRIME Minister James Marape has praised the Papua New Guinea Kickboxing Federation and its President, former World Champion Stanley Nandex, for using sport to transform the lives of young Papua New Guineans through discipline, respect and positive leadership. Speaking after attending a major nationa

item.title: NCD Women’s Council holds first AGM after 15 years
item.description: [The PNG Sun] The National Capital District Women’s Council has officially resumed operations after 15 years of inactivity. It held its first Annual General Meeting on Tuesday to restore a formal platform for women’s representation in the capital. The elected office holders were not announced yet. Related news: h

item.title: PMGH laik ol famili rausim bodi long mog hariap
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Sif Ekseketiv Opisa bilong Pot Mosbi Jeneral Haus Sik, Dokta Paki Molumi i singaut long ol pipel long go sekim mog na rausim dai bodi bilong ol famili na wantok bilong ol hariap, na karim i go planim. Mog i pulap na wanem ol bodi i stap longtaim bai haus sik i bungim olgeta na planim long wanpela bi

item.title: More complaints of illegal FCA logging
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Communities in remote Nakanai region of West New Britain say their forests are being illegally destroyed by a Malaysian logging company

item.description: [Bougainville News] One year on from the release of an independent investigation into Rio Tinto’s former Panguna mine in Bougainville, communities living with the ongoing environmental impact are calling on the company to urgently move towards funding solutions, particularly in areas identified … Continue reading →

item.description: [Inside PNG] PNG braces for an extraordinary dry season with looming water shortages and crop stress. Districts ordered to map local water sources. item.title: PNG Ready to Share Mining Experience as Solomon Islands Plans Resource Growth
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] Papua New Guinea has pledged to assist Solomon Islands develop a stronger mining industry by sharing its experience in resource governance and encouraging PNG companies to invest in future mining ventures across the neighbouring Melanesian nation. PNG Ready to Share Mining Experience as Solomon Isla

item.title: Proposed Polygamy Law in PNG
item.description: [Pawa TV] Polygamy in Papua New Guinea is rooted in tradition, but the government, recognising its impact on gender equality, plans to introduce laws banning it. Consultations will precede this reform. The post Proposed Polygamy Law in PNG first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: PNG Commerce Minister Denies Misuse Claims, Calls Social Media Posts Defamatory
item.description: [PNG Facts] Commerce and Industry Minister Win Bakri Daki Papua New Guinea's Commerce and Industry Minister Win Bakri Daki has strongly rejected allegations circulating on social media accusing him of misappropriating public funds, describing the posts as a deliberate attempt to damage his public standing ahead

item.description: [Post Courier] The National Airports Corporation (NAC) and AG Investments recently signed a PGK24.7 million contract in Port Moresby to begin the Domestic Terminal Expansion Project at Port Moresby International Airport. item.title: SP Brewery turns brewing by-product into Livestock feed through local partnership
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] WHAT was once considered a brewing by-product is set to become a valuable resource for Papua New Guinea’s livestock industry, following a new partnership between SP Brewery and local company Nature Calls Limited. The partnership, announced in Port Moresby on Wednesday, will see spent grain from SP B

item.title: Governor: NCDC and I have no power to evict settlement residents
item.description: [The PNG Sun] National Capital District Governor Powes Parkop has rejected social media claims that he is evicting settlement residents, stating that the National Capital District Commission has no legal authority to remove people from settlements. Speaking during the opening of the Erima Mambu Road last night, G

item.title: La Nina bai kam - gavman mas redi
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] National Weather Service (NWS) bilong PNG i tok 'La Nina' bai kam klostu nau tasol i no gat wanpela toksave i kam long gavman yet long wanem samting ol bai mekim o sapos ol i redi long wanem kain ol hevi dispela taim nogut bai nap long bringim.

item.description: [Act Now PNG] An international financial crackdown is underway in PNG with global intelligence agency INTERPOL

item.description: [Bougainville News] Bougainville Electoral Commissioner, Desmond Tsianai, today formally returned 45 writs comprising the Presidency, 38 single-member constituencies and the six reserved regional seats for women and Former Combatants. Tsianai formally handed the election writs to the outgoing Speaker, Simon Pentanu, of

item.title: Breadfruit is a Food Often Taken for Granted
item.description: [Inside PNG] NARI urges PNG to prioritize breadfruit as a key food security crop to protect local communities from severe El Niño droughts. The post Breadfruit is a Food Often Taken for Granted first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Breadfruit is a Food Often Taken for Granted appeared first on Inside PNG .

item.title: PNG Remote Communities to Benefit from Australia–Adventist Aviation Partnership
item.description: [PNG Facts] Australia has entered a new partnership with Adventist Aviation Services (AAS) to expand access to lifesaving health services for people living in some of Papua New Guinea's most isolated communities. The initiative will strengthen air transport services that provide emergency medical evacuations, d

item.title: Vanuatu on guard as drug traffick threat on the rise in the Pacific
item.description: [Post Courier] As illicit drug trafficking and transnational organised crime continue to spread across the Pacific, the Government of Vanuatu is stepping up efforts to protect its borders by convening its first National Summit on Border Security and Preparedness for Emerging Drug and HIV Threats next week. The sum

item.title: Hekari Women defeat Ba Women
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] HEKARI Women FC have defeated Ba Women FC 3-1 in Group A at OFC Women’s Champions League 2026 in Honiara, Solomon Islands yesterday. Hekari Women won 6-1 when the two sides met in the semi-finals of OFC Women’s Champions League 2025 last year in Tahiti but were forced to work much harder for victory

item.title: Governor Parkop urges women to reclaim their voice as NCD Women’s Council holds first AGM in 15 years
item.description: [The PNG Sun] NCD Governor Powes Parkop has called on women across Papua New Guinea to take a stronger role in leadership and decision-making as the NCD Women’s Council held its first Annual General Meeting in 15 years. Speaking at the AGM at Sir John Guise Indoor Complex on Tuesday, Governor Parkop described the

item.title: Call on Forest Minister and Board to explain failure to cancel FCA permits
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW! is calls on the Forest Minster and National Forest Board to explain why controversial FCA logging permits have not been cancelled.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : Could tourism lead Bougainville into the future ?
item.description: [Bougainville News] Bougainville has gone through many turbulent periods over the past 100 years, not least the Civil War of the 1990s, which claimed as many as 20,000 lives. The government is now looking to develop the economy and has its eye … Continue reading →

item.title: USP Concludes Laucala Graduation with a strong focus on Pacific Leadership and Legacies
item.description: [Education News PNG] The University of the South Pacific (USP) concluded its two-day graduation ceremonies at the Laucala Campus in Suva, Fiji, today, marking another important milestone as The post USP Concludes Laucala Graduation with a strong focus on Pacific Leadership and Legacies first appeared on Education News P

item.title: PNG Customs Busts Gold-Smuggling Operation
item.description: [Inside PNG] Nine Chinese nationals arrested in PNG's biggest gold-smuggling bust. 14.5kg of gold sand found on flight PX8. Strict bail granted. The post PNG Customs Busts Gold-Smuggling Operation first appeared on Inside PNG . The post PNG Customs Busts Gold-Smuggling Operation appeared first on Inside PNG .

item.title: PNG Chiefs project attracts overseas media as Matty Johns arrives
item.description: [PNG Facts] PNG's push towards becoming an NRL powerhouse continues to capture international attention, with Australian rugby league identity Matty Johns arriving in Port Moresby yesterday to cover the progress of the PNG Chiefs. Accompanied by a Fox League television crew, Johns' visit highlights the growing i

item.title: Workshop empowers CSOs to advance social accountability and launch PNG’s first citizens’ budget
item.description: [Post Courier] The Consultative Implementation and Monitoring Council (CIMC) recently joined civil society organisations (CSOs) and advocacy bodies for an intensive four-day workshop focused on social accountability and raising awareness about the importance of a Citizens’ Budget in Papua New Guinea. To access thi

item.title: Bougainville Takes Control of Village Courts System
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] THE Autonomous Region of Bougainville has completed a major transfer of its grassroots justice system, bringing 642 Village Courts and land mediation positions under the control of the Autonomous Bougainville Government (ABG). The move is the first large-scale function transfer under the Shaping Bou

item.title: St John staff member Jethro Philemon honored for decade of service
item.description: [The PNG Sun] National St John Ambulance PNG recognized Jethro Philemon for ten years of service during the organization’s 69th anniversary celebrations last week on St John Day in Port Moresby. He was honored for a career marked by leadership, sacrifice and dedication to emergency services. Mr. Philemon, who cur

item.title: Call on Forest Minister and Board to explain failure to cancel FCA permits
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW is calling on the Forest Minster and National Forest Board to explain why controversial FCA logging permits have not been cancelled

item.title: Chinese Investment Creates Over 10,000 PNG Jobs, Says Ambassador
item.description: [PNG Facts] Artistic Expression of China Town, Port Moresby Chinese investment in PNG has generated more than 10,000 direct jobs for Papua New Guineans, with businesses from China continuing to expand across a broad range of industries, according to Chinese Ambassador Yang Xiaoguang. The ambassador said the gro

item.title: K8 billion lost in illicit forestry activities under investigation
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Estimated K8.78 billion lost through illicit forestry activities and suspected money laundering schemes is now at the centre of four major investigations under Operation Arcadia, a multi-agency crackdown supported by INTERPOL.

item.title: PNG Public Servants Given 29 October Deadline to Resign for 2027 Election Bid
item.description: [PNG Facts] Public servants across PNG who plan to stand in the 2027 National General Election have been told they must resign from their government positions no later than 29 October 2026 to meet legal requirements. The deadline is outlined in a new Circular Instruction issued by the Department of Personnel Ma
